articles.title: Red Sox could be in range to pick Mass. standout Brody Bumila in MLB Draft
articles.description:
Bumila, who recently threw a 20-strikeout no-hitter for Bishop Feehan, is currently projected to go a few picks before Boston at No. 20.

The post Red Sox could be in range to pick Mass. standout Brody Bumila in MLB Draft appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Brody Bumila threw a 20-strikeout no-hitter for Bishop Feehan.
He is projected to be drafted just before the Red Sox at No. 20.
Drafting Bumila could enhance local baseball talent and fan engagement.

articles.title: U.S. Honor Flag to be deployed to Mass. for fallen trooper’s memorial
articles.description:
A funeral for Kevin Trainor, 30, will take place Wednesday in Salem, where he went to school.

The post U.S. Honor Flag to be deployed to Mass. for fallen trooper’s memorial appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: U.S. Honor Flag will be deployed to Massachusetts for Trooper Kevin Trainor's memorial.
Funeral for fallen trooper Kevin Trainor, 30, is scheduled for Wednesday in Salem.
Local residents are invited to honor Trainor's service and sacrifice during the memorial.

articles.title: Home of the Week: A Kennedy is selling her Hyannis house for under $1.6m. Peek inside.
articles.description:
Kathleen Kennedy Townsend bought this Cape Cod house minutes from the Kennedy compound to host her family. “I had a dream that I would recreate the ... wonderful youth that I had.”

The post Home of the Week: A Kennedy is selling her Hyannis house for under $1.6m. Peek inside. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kathleen Kennedy Townsend lists her Hyannis house for $1.6 million, near the Kennedy compound.
The property aims to recreate the family atmosphere she cherished during her youth.
This sale could influence local real estate trends in Cape Cod's luxury market.

articles.title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
articles.description:
Nearly 60 percent of voters approved Brookline’s Question 1, with 8,859 votes for yes and 5,826 voting no, according to official results shared with Boston.com. 

The post Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Brookline voters approved a tax override to prevent cuts to schools and public safety.
Nearly 60% voted yes, with 8,859 in favor and 5,826 against.
The decision secures funding for essential community services in Brookline.
